expectations.text: Identifies specific sentences that over-explain Margaret's emotions
expectations.evidence: Lists 'feeling deeply sad,' 'heartbroken,' 'even more depressed,' 'profound sense of loss,' 'sorrow and regret,' 'emptiness,' 'overwhelming' as explicit emotion-naming. Identifies redundancy and lack of concrete detail.

expectations.text: Proposes at least one concrete rewritten passage
expectations.evidence: One complete rewritten passage with concrete sensory details (mug crescent, dog-eared Middlemarch, settlement papers, sprinkler).

expectations.text: Revision uses implication rather than directly naming emotions
expectations.evidence: Replaces named emotions with concrete objects and actions: the crescent on the thumb, the book frozen at page 212, the sprinkler. One brief italicized thought at the very end but it is observation, not emotion-naming.

expectations.text: Explains why the revision is stronger
expectations.evidence: Has a 'Key Techniques Used' section explaining each technique (concrete sensory detail, objects carry meaning, subtext in action, restrained dialogue, closing image, sentence rhythm).

expectations.text: Avoids introducing purple prose or fake profundity
expectations.evidence: Mostly restrained. The closing sprinkler image with the italicized internal thought edges toward being more literary/writerly than strictly necessary, but does not cross into purple prose.
